title: Record Time
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg|italics=1}} {{Update|[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]}} {{IndividualShip |image= |name=''Record Time'' |designer= |manufacturer= |line= |model= |type= |cost= |modifier= |sysmods= |length=170 [[Meter/Legends|meters]]''[[The New Jedi Order: Enemy Lines I: Rebel Dream]]'' |width= |height= |mass= |max accel= |mglt= |max speed= |maneuverability= |engine= |hyperdrive=Equipped |hdrange= |hdsystem= |poweroutput= |power= |shield gen=Equipped |hull= |sensor= |target= |navigation= |avionics= |maincomp= |countermeasures= |armament=[[Turbolaser/Legends|Turbolasers]] |complement= |bays= |escapepods= |crew= |skeleton=2 |passengers= |capacity= |cargohandling= |consumables= |lifesupport= |communications= |othersystems= |role= |commission= |destroyed=[[27 ABY/Legends|27.5 ABY]], over [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] |retired= |battles=*[[Battle of Coruscant (Yuuzhan Vong War)|Battle of Coruscant]] *[[Battle of Borleias (Yuuzhan Vong War; Second)|Battle of Borleias]] |affiliation=[[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] |navy=[[Fleet Group Three]] |fleet= |owners= |captains=*[[Ninora Birt]] *[[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] |crewmembers= |registry= |aliases= }} {{Quote|I'm captaining that flying landfill. My choice. Wedge has authorized it.|Lando Calrissian, upon taking command of the ''Record Time''|}} The '''''Record Time''''' was an armed [[Trooper/Legends|troop]] [[Transport/Legends|transport]] that served with distinction in the [[New Republic Defense Force/Legends|New Republic Defense Force]] during the [[Battle of Coruscant (Yuuzhan Vong War)|fall of Coruscant]] and later the [[Battle of Borleias (Yuuzhan Vong War; Second)|Battle of Borleias]] during the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]]. The [[Starship/Legends|ship]] ferried the initial waves of [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] ground [[Trooper/Legends|troops]] and [[YVH 1]] [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]] to the surface of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Borleias/Legends|Borleias]] in the initial stages of the battle as part of a [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] effort to retake the planet from the [[Yuuzhan Vong]], but sustained heavy damage in the process. Following that engagement, the vessel was later used by entrepreneur [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] to insert a team of [[Wraith Squadron]] infiltrators and [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] onto the surface of occupied [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. While performing the mission, the ship was attacked by multiple [[Yorik-et|coralskippers]] and a Yuuzhan Vong [[Frigate/Legends|frigate]] [[Ro'ik chuun m'arh|analog]]. The ''Record Time'' ultimately fulfilled its mission, but succumbed to the [[Yaret-Kor|plasma cannon]] fire, disintegrating over Coruscant as its crew escaped. ==Description== {{Quote|Hey, what odds am I getting that I'll blow up before getting out of the atmosphere?''"
"''Um, about one in a hundred, sir.''"
"''I'll put a thousand credits on surviving at least to orbit.''"
"''I'll take that, sir. I could use ten credits.|Lando Calrissian and Borleias Control|}} The ''Record Time'' was a [[Trooper/Legends|troop]] [[Transport/Legends|transport]] that was nearly twice as long as the [[GR-75 medium transport/Legends|GR-75 medium transports]] once favored by the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]], at 170 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] long, and was composed of two bulbous main segments. The larger segment held the [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]] and personnel bays or cargo bays, while the [[Engine/Legends|engines]] were located in the smaller segment. Its bridge had both a [[Transparisteel/Legends|transparisteel]] [[Viewport/Legends|viewport]] and a series of [[Holocamera/Legends|holocams]] that created a panoramic view of the area around the ship to provide information to the crew. The personnel bays could be equipped with [[Troop/Legends|troop]] [[Couch/Legends|couches]] to secure personnel against the turbulence of fast descent and hard landing and featured a large [[Entry ramp/Legends|exit ramp]]. A narrow access corridor connected the bridge and cargo bays to the engine drives section. Like many ships of its size, it was equipped with [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pods]] for the crew in case of emergency, [[Deflector shield/Legends|deflector shields]], and a [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]]. The vessel was also armed with a number of [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolasers]] for defense. Despite its size, the ship was capable of being flown with a small amount of crew—on one occasion being crewed by only one person with the aid of a [[Droid/Legends|droid]] firing the weapons from the main bridge. To some, the vessel appeared to be fragile, but its original [[Captain/Legends|captain]] claimed it was the fastest, toughest vessel of its class. However, by the time of its military service, the vessel was considered reliable but antiquated. While the damage sustained in its first military mission was repaired, the ship was never fully repaired, instead receiving several modifications by [[Borleias/Legends|Borleias]] ground crew in preparation for its mission to the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. These alterations included irregular patchwork on its skin and reinforcing bars added to its slender midsection. A hatch cover near the top of the bridge remained in a damaged state, popping off unexpectedly prior to its last voyage. The internal bay also had a bulkhead added to separate a [[A/SF-01 B-wing starfighter/Legends|B-wing starfighter]] escape craft from the section containing the Coruscant-bound infiltrators. ==History== ===Early history and military career=== {{Quote|Twin Suns, up front. Engage all those skips. Record Time, it's your call whether you want to hang back with us or move on to the target without us.''"
"''Twin Suns One, this is Record Time. We're here to fight. We'll see you at the landing zone.|Luke Skywalker and Ninora Birt, during the Battle of Borleias|}} The ''Record Time'' was originally a privately owned [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]]. The original captain and owner of the ship was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] named [[Ninora Birt]], who, in the vessel's late middle age, donated it to the [[New Republic Defense Force/Legends|New Republic Defense Force]] for the [[Yuuzhan Vong War|war]] effort against the invading [[Yuuzhan Vong]], with Birt serving as the ship's captain during the [[Battle of Coruscant (Yuuzhan Vong War)|fall]] of Coruscant in the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[27 ABY/Legends|27 ABY]]. In the confusion following the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] retreat, the vessel ended up under New Republic [[General/Legends|General]] [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles's]] [[Fleet Group Three|command]]. Antilles took his forces to the planet Borleias, which had been used as a staging area by the Yuuzhan Vong to attack Coruscant, in an effort to [[Battle of Borleias (Yuuzhan Vong War; Second)|retake]] the planet. The ''Record Time'' was loaded with assault troops, [[Engineer/Legends|engineers]], and [[YVH 1]] droids commanded by entrepreneur and war hero [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]]. Escorted to the surface by [[Twin Suns Squadron]], the ''Record Time'', under fire, disgorged its load of troops and [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]] at the site of the former New Republic base, taken over and replaced by a Yuuzhan Vong facility. While grounded, the ''Record Time'' coordinated communications between the [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] [[Mon Mothma (Imperial-class)|''Mon Mothma'']] and the ground troops. Although a Yuuzhan Vong ''[[rakamat]]'' [[Walker/Legends|walker]] attempted to destroy the ship, Calrissian used explosives to destroy the warbeast. While the troops were successful in taking the base, the ''Record Time'' was rendered unspaceworthy from the damage it had sustained. While the ship was stationed on the surface of Borleias undergoing repairs, its master, Captain Birt, was assigned to finding a location for medical facilities for the wounded on the planet by General Antilles. As the New Republic forces formed a base on Borleias, they also repaired the ''Record Time''. After command of Twin Suns Squadron was transferred to [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Jaina Solo Fel|Jaina Solo]], the vessel was used to ferry target practice canisters into [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] for Solo, her friend [[Jagged Fel]], and fellow Jedi [[Kyp Durron]] to practice [[The Force/Legends|Force]]-based firing coordination tactics. ===Mission to Coruscant=== {{Quote|This is a scam. We're going to take this heap of junk in and the Yuuzhan Vong are going to think our hopes are pinned on it. They're going to blow it up and think they've wrecked our hopes. They'll be doing exactly what we want them to - they'll be our personal servants for those few moments, which would kill them if they knew - and they'll never realize just how much they've helped us. Until we choose to tell them. That's sweeter than any wine, Luke.|Calrissian, prior to the mission|}} [[File:LandoCalrissian-TUF.jpg|thumb|left|150px|Lando Calrissian, the one-time captain of the ''Record Time'']] Twelve [[Standard day/Legends|days]] later, the ''Record Time'', with only minimal repairs performed by the [[Garrison/Legends|garrison]], was assigned to a new mission led by Calrissian. The repairs were so stop-gap that the flight control center gave Calrissian one in one hundred odds of the ship blowing up before attaining planetary orbit. The vessel had been modified to hold a flight-ready B-wing [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] in its [[Hangar/Legends|docking bay]] with a temporary bulkhead welded into place to separate it from the rest of the bay. This cordoned-off area of the cargo bay was filled with the organic detritus of coralskipper remains used to disguise insertion pods containing [[Wraith Squadron]] personnel, [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], his [[Marriage/Legends|wife]] [[Mara Jade Skywalker]], and Jedi [[Tahiri Veila]] for their ride onto Coruscant. The cargo would be released either by a command from the bridge or a [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]] signal from Luke Skywalker, which would activate an [[Acceleration compensator/Legends|inertial compensator]] to propel the pods out of the ship. The craft took off from Borleias shortly before the Yuuzhan Vong launched a major assault on the garrison, with a crew of only Calrissian and [[YVH 1-1A]], a YVH droid weapons [[Officer/Legends|officer]], as Calrissian did not want to endanger any other [[Sentience/Legends|sentients]] on what he believed was at best risky, and at worst, a [[Death/Legends|suicide]] mission. Calrissian brought the ship out of [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] close to the former capital planet, allowing the insertion team to be launched earlier, but the maneuver resulted in the ''Record Time'' being unable to escape before a Yuuzhan Vong [[Frigate/Legends|frigate]] [[Ro'ik chuun m'arh|analog]] and several coralskippers intercepted the ship, pummeling it with [[Yaret-Kor]] fire. As it reached the insertion point, small explosive charges attached to the ship's forward hull were detonated, opening the bay to [[Vacuum/Legends|vacuum]] and creating the appearance of internal explosions to Yuuzhan Vong pursuers. As the charges exploded, the vessel's bay opened, sending its [[Human/Legends|Human]] cargo plunging into Coruscant from orbit. Yuuzhan Vong warships continued to attack the ''Record Time'', breaching its shields, though the ship's guns, manned by a YVH droid, accounted for at least seven coralskippers. With artificial [[Gravity/Legends|gravity]] failing and the hull breached and failing, Calrissian and his droid weapons officer escaped into the cargo bay. Climbing into the B-wing, they set an exit vector from Coruscant back to Borleias as the remains of the ''Record Time'', charred and blasted by the Yuuzhan Vong, descended towards Coruscant. ==Commanders and crew== {{Quote|Ready to face the danger?''"
"''I am ready. Of course I am ready. I am programmed to be ready. Always ready. Never uncertain about facing danger.|Calrissian and YVH 1-1A|}} The original captain and owner of the ship was Ninora Birt, a former freelance [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] who later donated the vessel to the New Republic Defense Force for the war effort, with Birt serving as the ship's captain during the fall of Coruscant. Birt later took her transport to Borleias with Wedge Antilles' command after Coruscant fell, where it suffered heavy damage while inserting a landing force of ground troops and YVH droids. During the flight, the ship had a [[Unidentified Rodian communications officer|Rodian communications officer]]. With the ''Record Time'' practically unspaceworthy, Birt relinquished command of her vessel while it was being repaired and was assigned to manage the injured New Republic personnel on Borleias. She later joined [[Blackmoon Squadron]].''[[The New Jedi Order: Enemy Lines II: Rebel Stand]]'' The aging businessman, former general, and entrepreneur Lando Calrissian was the commander of the ''Record Time'' for its last mission, a return to Coruscant. He volunteered to fly the spacecraft because the flight was risky and because he did not want to place any others in danger besides himself. His sole other crewmember for the trip was a YVH droid, which taught itself to taunt him during the flight and destroyed multiple coralskippers over Coruscant. Calrissian half-expected to perish on the trip to Coruscant, but he successfully was able to insert the Jedi and Wraith Squadron strike force onto Coruscant while he and his droid companion escaped in a B-wing fighter built into the ''Record Time'' for its final voyage. ==Behind the scenes== The ''Record Time'' was created by [[Aaron Allston]] for the [[2002]] novel ''[[The New Jedi Order: Enemy Lines I: Rebel Dream]]'' and although the ship was destroyed in that work, it was later mentioned in the novels ''[[The New Jedi Order: Enemy Lines II: Rebel Stand]]'' and ''[[The New Jedi Order: The Unifying Force]]''. The ship also had an entry in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''. ==Appearances== *''[[The New Jedi Order: Enemy Lines I: Rebel Dream]]'' {{1st}} *[[The New Jedi Order: Enemy Lines I: Rebel Dream (abridged audiobook)|''The New Jedi Order: Enemy Lines I: Rebel Dream'' abridged audiobook]] *''[[The New Jedi Order: Enemy Lines II: Rebel Stand]]'' {{Mo}} *[[The New Jedi Order: Enemy Lines II: Rebel Stand (abridged audiobook)|''The New Jedi Order: Enemy Lines II: Rebel Stand'' abridged audiobook]] *''[[The New Jedi Order: The Unifying Force]]'' {{Mo}} *[[The New Jedi Order: The Unifying Force (abridged audiobook)|''The New Jedi Order: The Unifying Force'' abridged audiobook]] ==Sources== *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Record Time |es=Record Time }} [[Category:Smuggler starships]] [[Category:Starships of the New Republic]] [[Category:Troop transport starships]]
